jokes.text: Look... I am throwing a farewell party for my best friend, Ann Perkins. And if she casually mentioned 3 years ago that she thought indoor fireworks would be cool then guess what, you're gonna give me indoor fireworks.
jokes.setup: Leslie making a seemingly normal party planning request
jokes.punchline: Revealing the absurd level of detail and commitment to fulfilling a casual 3-year-old comment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character comedy - her obsessive attention to friends' casual comments and over-the-top party planning. The escalation from normal request to 3-year callback is expertly structured.

jokes.text: That promise launched a friendship so grand it takes 103 scrapbooks to capture it.
jokes.setup: Reference to documenting a friendship
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific number of 103 scrapbooks
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific absurdism. The specific number '103' makes it funnier than just 'many' - shows Leslie's obsessive documentation habits.

jokes.text: - Damn, that's really good. - Right? There's gonna be a lot of these at the party. - You should come by. - I kno-- I get it. I know what you're doing. And I still--is there a little crunch in there? - Hazelnut. - Ooh.
jokes.setup: Harold trying to resist Leslie's party invitation and maintain professional boundaries
jokes.punchline: His resolve completely crumbling over a good ice cream sandwich, unable to resist asking about ingredients
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation structure showing how Leslie's treat strategy works even on resistant people. The progression from resistance to curiosity about hazelnuts is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Ann's leaving town and saying painful good-byes to loved ones. [cut to April] Greatest day of my life!
jokes.setup: Setup about painful goodbyes to loved ones
jokes.punchline: April's genuine excitement about Ann leaving
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment with excellent editing structure. The stark contrast between the setup and her genuine joy is perfectly timed and completely in character.

jokes.text: Hey, that box has my name on it. Hey, this one has my name on it. This one has plates' name on it.
jokes.setup: Characters finding boxes with their names
jokes.punchline: Jerry finding a box literally labeled 'plates'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that builds to Jerry's typical misfortune - he gets the box meant for actual plates rather than a personalized gift.

jokes.text: Fun pun alert. A bronzed hamburger.
jokes.setup: Leslie announcing her own pun
jokes.punchline: The self-aware announcement of a mediocre pun
jokes.explanation: Mild meta-humor that's very Leslie-like but not particularly strong. The self-awareness of announcing a weak pun is the main comedic element.

jokes.text: I would have preferred a regular hamburger for eating, but this is very nice.
jokes.setup: Receiving a bronzed hamburger as a gift
jokes.punchline: Ron's practical assessment that he'd prefer an edible version
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron deadpan delivery. His practical mindset contrasting with Leslie's sentimental gift-giving is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: 2008 Indiana state audit spreadsheets? Man, that year was insane! That was the year you went from being an auditor to a legend.
jokes.setup: Finding mundane government spreadsheets in a gift box
jokes.punchline: Leslie's genuine excitement about audit spreadsheets and treating bureaucratic work as legendary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character comedy - her ability to find excitement in the most mundane government work. The 'auditor to legend' line is particularly funny.

jokes.text: I did have to go to a very seedy location to find it, but now I know where to go to place a bet on horse fighting.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining how she obtained banned Snakejuice
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning learning about illegal horse fighting gambling
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist escalation where Leslie's innocent quest leads to discovering illegal animal gambling. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Tell Ann about the groundbreaking, frame Ann for murder, release a deadly virus into the water supply.
jokes.setup: Leslie asking Tom to repeat back what NOT to mention to Ann
jokes.punchline: Tom's completely wrong and increasingly absurd interpretation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation joke showing Tom's selective listening. The progression from groundbreaking to murder to bioterrorism is well-structured absurdism.

jokes.text: Why is there a New Year's Eve countdown clock and the Easter bunny? I don't know when we're gonna be able to visit each other, so just to be safe this party is a celebration of every event that's gonna happen this year.
jokes.setup: Ann noticing random holiday decorations together
jokes.punchline: Leslie's over-the-top solution of combining every possible celebration into one party
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-planning taken to its logical extreme. The visual absurdity of mixed holiday decorations supports the character-driven humor.

jokes.text: Ain't no party like a Leslie Knope party 'cause a Leslie Knope party is actually 30 parties.
jokes.setup: Riff on the famous rap lyric format
jokes.punchline: The mathematical reality of Leslie's over-planning
jokes.explanation: Excellent adaptation of a familiar format with perfect economy. Captures both Leslie's party-planning excess and Tom's way of speaking.

jokes.text: This is just a bunch of bras and knives. And loose onions.
jokes.setup: Leslie claiming she packed a bag for Ann
jokes.punchline: The completely random and impractical items Leslie chose
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor with the escalation from underwear to weapons to produce. 'Loose onions' is a particularly funny specific detail.

jokes.text: Well, that card's for three pans. Either way, this is great.
jokes.setup: Being told about a gift card from 'Pots and Pans'
jokes.punchline: Chris's enthusiastic acceptance despite the clear limitation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's relentless positivity even when receiving clearly inadequate gifts. His enthusiasm is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: Carob cookies and berries are literally my favorite dessert alternative.
jokes.setup: Reference to health-conscious but unappetizing dessert options
jokes.punchline: Chris's genuine enthusiasm for what sounds terrible to normal people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris character comedy - his health obsession making him genuinely excited about what others would consider punishment food.

jokes.text: I also shook his hand. Twice. Anything more than that would be excessive.
jokes.setup: Discussing what gifts they got Chris
jokes.punchline: Ben's characteristically awkward and minimal social gesture
jokes.explanation: Great Ben character moment showing his social awkwardness and overthinking. The specific 'twice' and treating it as generous is perfectly awkward.

jokes.text: I say we start with the three 'C's,' cashmere, concert tickets, caboodles of cash. I feel like those are perfect gifts for you. Those are perfect gifts for anyone. Ugh, I wish you guys were Donna!
jokes.setup: Tom suggesting gift ideas
jokes.punchline: Andy's innocent observation that Tom is basically describing what Tom himself would want, leading to Tom's frustrated realization
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay showing Tom's self-centeredness and Andy's unexpected insight. The escalation to wishing for Donna is a nice payoff.

jokes.text: But now that you're officially out of the dating pool, you know I'm gonna run this town, right? Ooh. Oh, yeah, you do not wanna go down that road. Trust me, I tried.
jokes.setup: Donna claiming she'll dominate the Pawnee dating scene
jokes.punchline: Tom's failed attempt to interject about his own dating prowess, immediately shot down
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamics showing Tom's delusions about his dating success and the women's realistic assessment. The immediate shutdown is well-timed.

jokes.text: There's also a sign that says 'Especially Leslie Knope.' Well, Leslie Knope is a very common name. There's a professor in Copenhagen named Leslie Knope. They probably mean him. Do they mean the professor? Yeah, there's a picture of you also.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to rationalize away increasingly specific evidence that she's banned
jokes.punchline: The final reveal that there's literally a picture of her
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing Leslie's denial and Ben's patient delivery of increasingly damning evidence. The Copenhagen professor is a great absurd detail.

jokes.text: Holy mother of Malia! And Sasha. I love them both equally.
jokes.setup: Leslie starting to curse in frustration
jokes.punchline: Converting her curse into a reference to Obama's daughters with an immediate clarification of equal love
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - her political correctness even extends to her cursing, and her need to clarify equal treatment of the Obama daughters is hilariously in character.

jokes.text: If Chris gets cranky around noon, just stuff some chia seeds into a fig--works every time.
jokes.setup: Ann giving care instructions for Chris
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and health-obsessed solution to his mood problems
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Chris's health obsessions. The specific 'chia seeds into a fig' is absurd enough to be funny while still believable for Chris.

jokes.text: I told him that One Headlight By the Wallflowers isn't 'dancing' music and he said 'Not with that attitude.'
jokes.setup: Ann explaining Chris's whereabouts
jokes.punchline: Chris's relentlessly positive response to criticism of his music choice
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Chris's ability to find positivity in anything. The specific song choice and his response are perfectly in character and quotable.

jokes.text: We used to date. Yeah, we lived together for two years. I know, it's crazy, right? Anyways, you're the best. Have fun in Mexico.
jokes.setup: Andy suddenly remembering his relationship with Ann
jokes.punchline: His casual dismissal of their two-year relationship and getting her destination wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy character moment - his childlike ability to treat serious relationships as casual memories, plus getting basic facts wrong. Very in character.

jokes.text: I'm just so scared that this means that Ann won't really leave.
jokes.setup: April appearing upset about the groundbreaking complications
jokes.punchline: Revealing her 'fear' is actually that Ann might not leave
jokes.explanation: Great April character consistency - maintaining her anti-Ann stance while pretending concern. The misdirection works perfectly with her character.

jokes.text: Maybe one day years from now when we're both old and Chris is dead, you'll run into me somewhere. I'll be opening my latest mega club. I'll see you there on the street by yourself and I'll tell the bouncer/my bodyguard, 'Hey, that woman's way too old to get into this club, but why don't you let her in the back?'
jokes.setup: Tom giving a sentimental goodbye to Ann
jokes.punchline: His 'romantic' future scenario is actually insulting and involves Chris dying
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tom character comedy - his attempt at romance becomes inadvertently insulting and weirdly specific. The casual mention of Chris's death and age-shaming is perfectly Tom.

jokes.text: I have enjoyed parts of our time together.
jokes.setup: Ron giving what should be a heartfelt goodbye
jokes.punchline: His characteristically minimal and qualified endorsement
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment - the word 'parts' makes this hilariously underwhelming while still being the most emotion we'd expect from him. Great economy.

jokes.text: This is my contribution. It says 'Ron.'
jokes.setup: Ron presenting his gift contribution
jokes.punchline: His minimal effort - apparently just writing his own name on something
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron minimal effort humor. The deadpan delivery and complete lack of effort while still technically contributing is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Dark forces, arise! Okay, I didn't plan what we were all gonna say. That's my bad.
jokes.setup: Group doing a team cheer before their mission
jokes.punchline: Tom's random and inappropriate battle cry followed by awkward acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment with Tom's inappropriate gothic reference in a team-building moment, followed by his realistic acknowledgment of poor planning.

jokes.text: Just think of us as your genies who can give you anything you want. Nothing gross.
jokes.setup: Leslie offering to grant Harold's wishes
jokes.punchline: Her immediate practical limitation on the genie metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment showing her enthusiasm tempered by practical boundaries. The quick qualifier is realistic and funny.

jokes.text: Super fizzy fruity pop. Sorry? Sweetums used to stock our office with free sodas as a promotional thing, but that Kathryn Pinewood lady just took 'em away.
jokes.setup: Harold having the power to block Leslie's important project
jokes.punchline: His demand being about getting free soda back
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between the epic scope of Leslie's project and Harold's petty, mundane concern. The phrase 'super fizzy fruity pop' is childlike and funny.

jokes.text: Kathryn Pinewood is the person on earth who hates me the most. Well, luckily, you're with the person on earth who loves you the most.
jokes.setup: Leslie expressing her fear about facing Kathryn
jokes.punchline: Ann's sweet response that perfectly balances Leslie's dramatic statement
jokes.explanation: Sweet friendship moment with good structure - Ann's response perfectly matches Leslie's dramatic energy.

jokes.text: Sorry, can't help you ladies. I don't have time for disgraced former politicians and unmarried preggos.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ann asking for Kathryn's help
jokes.punchline: Kathryn's casually cruel and specific insults
jokes.explanation: Good character-consistent cruelty from Kathryn. The specific insults 'disgraced former politicians and unmarried preggos' show her casual meanness.

jokes.text: Yeah, Pinewood, that's right. I'm pregnant and I have you in a headlock. And if you try to fight back, I will sue you.
jokes.setup: Ann being insulted by Kathryn
jokes.punchline: Sweet Ann using her pregnancy as a weapon for physical intimidation
jokes.explanation: Excellent character subversion - usually gentle Ann using pregnancy as tactical advantage in physical confrontation. The legal threat makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: When is this town gonna realize that I'm more than a former high school basketball star? I don't think this new sports drink is even about basketball. It's called 'Sweetums dunktastic three-point B-ball blast' the slogan is 'It's all about basketball.'
jokes.setup: Detlef complaining about being typecast as a basketball player
jokes.punchline: Leslie pointing out the drink is literally entirely about basketball
jokes.explanation: Great irony joke with Detlef's denial immediately contradicted by the obviously basketball-focused product. The absurdly basketball-heavy name makes it funnier.

jokes.text: There is such a thing as journalistic integrity, and it is something that I have as a journalist with integrity. It's really important, Perd. Please? Come on. For old time's sake. Well, the story of that appeal is it was heartfelt. And in response to that appeal, I respond: Okay.
jokes.setup: Perd maintaining journalistic integrity
jokes.punchline: His immediate abandonment of principles when Ann asks nicely
jokes.explanation: Good Perd character moment showing his pompous language and easy manipulation. The formal phrasing of giving in is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: It was during that 'explore my freedom as a single woman' phase. We just had a drink. And at the end he said, 'I am going to kiss you now by putting my mouth on your mouth.' And then I ran away.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining her date with Perd
jokes.punchline: Perd's robotic announcement of his romantic intentions
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor with Perd's news-anchor formality applied to romance. The clinical description 'putting my mouth on your mouth' is hilariously awkward and quotable.

jokes.text: We tell him we had the perfect gift, but we were robbed at gunpoint. 'Chris, he had a gun. He took all the gifts.' Now Chris feels bad, he's buying us gifts. Next thing you know, Tommy's got a new watch.
jokes.setup: Tom proposing to lie about why their gifts are inadequate
jokes.punchline: The elaborate con ending with Tom getting personal benefit
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment showing his scheming nature and self-interest. The escalation from lie to personal gain is well-structured and in character.

jokes.text: Ask not what your country can do for you. From Family Guy, right?
jokes.setup: Andy quoting JFK
jokes.punchline: His wrong attribution to Family Guy
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy ignorance humor - attributing a famous presidential quote to a cartoon. Simple but effective character-consistent joke.

jokes.text: 'Ask not what your country can do for you.' From Family Guy, right?
jokes.setup: Chris quoting JFK
jokes.punchline: Andy attributing the famous quote to Family Guy
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy ignorance joke. His reference point for famous quotes being cartoons is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: And just the fact that you feel bad about your bag of nothing proves that.
jokes.setup: Chris explaining why their friendship matters
jokes.punchline: His sincere reference to their literally empty gift bag
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chris's ability to find meaning in anything, even acknowledging their terrible gifts while staying positive.

jokes.text: Harold, your tiny brain could not understand this, but that is the best compliment you could ever give the two women standing in front of you.
jokes.setup: Harold calling Leslie and Ann pains in the ass
jokes.punchline: Leslie's elaborate reframe of the insult as a compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie character moment showing her ability to reframe criticism positively while also insulting Harold's intelligence. Very in character.

jokes.text: You have to hiss really loud and stomp your feet so they'll leave you alone. And if one comes close, make yourself really large like a puffer fish.
jokes.setup: April giving raccoon advice
jokes.punchline: Her bizarre combination of animal behavior strategies
jokes.explanation: Good April weird knowledge moment. The mixed animal defense strategies are appropriately absurd.

jokes.text: You have to hiss really loud and stomp your feet so they'll leave you alone. And if one comes close, make yourself really large like a puffer fish.
jokes.setup: April giving advice about raccoons
jokes.punchline: Her ridiculous anti-raccoon strategy involving puffer fish behavior
jokes.explanation: Good April absurdist humor with her matter-of-fact delivery of ridiculous advice. The puffer fish comparison is unexpectedly specific and funny.

jokes.text: Well, Michigan has wolverines. Maybe they'll eat your toes.
jokes.setup: Discussing things Ann won't miss about Pawnee
jokes.punchline: April's casual suggestion that Michigan wildlife will harm Ann
jokes.explanation: Good April character moment with her deadpan delivery of morbid possibilities. The specific 'eat your toes' is darkly funny and in character.

jokes.text: My going-away present to you will be that I do not make you audibly say that you love me.
jokes.setup: April quietly saying she loves Ann
jokes.punchline: Ann's merciful decision not to force April to repeat it loudly
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Ann's understanding of April's emotional limitations. The framing as a 'gift' is perfect and shows their relationship growth.

jokes.text: It's quarter-sawn zebra wood adjoined by floating tenons to the black walnut corner posts. Finished with a wiping varnish that's a secret Swanson family recipe.
jokes.setup: Ron describing the wooden box he made
jokes.punchline: His incredibly detailed and technical woodworking specifications
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron character moment showcasing his craftsmanship expertise. The technical jargon contrasts beautifully with everyone else's simple gifts.

jokes.text: That's Garry Jerry Larry Gergich Gengurch. I burned in all my names. I really--I didn't know what to do.
jokes.setup: Someone asking about the long initials G-J-L-G-G
jokes.punchline: Jerry's confused explanation of burning in all his various name mistakes
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jerry's name confusion running gag. His genuine uncertainty about which name to use is both pathetic and endearing.

jokes.text: Salt water will warp the wood. So keep your tears in your eyes where they belong.
jokes.setup: Chris getting emotional about the thoughtful gift
jokes.punchline: Ron's practical concern about tears damaging his craftsmanship
jokes.explanation: Excellent Ron character moment - his practical craftsman concerns overriding emotional moments. Perfect economy and quotable delivery. Classic Ron.

jokes.text: But most importantly, you taught me that I can't pull off a tulip skirt. It's just not your shape.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing meaningful things Ann taught her
jokes.punchline: The final lesson being about unflattering clothing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where meaningful life lessons end with practical fashion advice. The specific 'tulip skirt' detail makes it funnier and shows their friendship.

jokes.text: But most importantly, you taught me that I can't pull off a tulip skirt. It's just not your shape.
jokes.setup: Leslie giving a heartfelt speech about what Ann taught her
jokes.punchline: Ending with the practical fashion advice about tulip skirts
jokes.explanation: Good structure mixing heartfelt sentiment with practical fashion advice. The anticlimax works well.

jokes.text: All right, I got my park. That's all I wanted from you. Friendship over.
jokes.setup: Completing the groundbreaking ceremony
jokes.punchline: Ann jokingly suggesting their friendship was just transactional
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann can match Leslie's intensity with humor. The deadpan delivery of ending their friendship is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: All right, I got my park. That's all I wanted from you. Friendship over.
jokes.setup: Ann and Leslie completing the groundbreaking
jokes.punchline: Ann jokingly dismissing their friendship as purely transactional
jokes.explanation: Good callback to their original meeting. Ann's deadpan delivery makes the joke work.

jokes.text: Shotgun! I called shotgun! You're all nailed. I call sitting on your lap. You can't--I--double shotgun! We call double shotgun! How come people don't do that more often? It's illegal.
jokes.setup: People calling shotgun for the car ride
jokes.punchline: The escalating chaos of car seating rules ending with it being illegal
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the group's childlike behavior and chaos. The final 'it's illegal' is a nice absurdist button on the sequence.

jokes.text: Shotgun! I called shotgun! You're all nailed. I call sitting on your lap. You can't--I--double shotgun! We call double shotgun!
jokes.setup: Group getting into cars for breakfast
jokes.punchline: The escalating and increasingly absurd shotgun calls
jokes.explanation: Fun escalation comedy with good group dynamic, though not particularly sophisticated.
